template_metadata.purpose: AI-executable template with step-level contracts for mechanically verifiable project plans
breaking_changes: Added step-level contracts with inputs/outputs/invariants
Added step_id tracking throughout pipeline
Added step evidence requirements
Added step-level file scoping
Introduced multi-level planning hierarchy and pipeline boundary contract
Introduced pattern-first execution definitions with execution_patterns section
Introduced mutation model for workstream decomposition
Added optional task intake model for continuous issue intake
Added traceability mapping to link requirements to modules, components and execution packets
Added execution_baseline with git_state and artifact_root_baseline for drift detection
Added concurrency_control for global and group-level concurrency limits
Added rollback_plan with failure_mode_to_recovery_mapping and fail_if_no_recovery_path
Added evidence_requirements with audit_trail and observable_success_requirements
Added lifecycle_states with run_status_states and mutation_ledger_states sub-machines
Added state_transitions with run_status_allowed, mutation_ledger_allowed, lock and atomicity controls
Added reconciliation_states, canonicalization_contract, merge_policy with manual_review_triggers
Added platform_profile for cross-platform support
Added protected_paths global section for RL-3 Protected Paths Inviolate
Added run_artifact_policy global section for I-3 Run Directory Containment
Added anti_pattern_detection global section for AP-1 Known Failures Detected
Added run_status_contract global section for C-2 Run Status Coherence
Added mutation_ledger global section for C-3 Ledger Integrity
Extended execution_patterns with pattern_validation and guardrail_policy for PG-1/PG-2/PG-3
Extended worktree_isolation with preflight and post_run_verification for I-2 Clean State Entry
Extended traceability with gap_to_execution_packet and gap_closure_validation for C-1
Extended step_contracts with pattern_id, requires_manual_review, runtime_limits, audit_fields, failure_logging
Added referenced_context_artifacts for external authoritative implementation context
Added artifact_intent_manifest to predeclare intended file mutations and deliverables
Added planned_registry_projection to predeclare stable artifact metadata before execution
Added validation gates for context binding, artifact intent completeness, intent-vs-output reconciliation, and registry projection reconciliation
Added planned_vs_observed separation rules for artifact lifecycle and metadata
Added abstraction_governance as a mandatory scoped control surface for contract-first boundaries and the contracts-are-abstractions law.
Added path_abstraction_contract requiring governed paths to use path keys, path registry resolution, and hardcoded-path index enforcement.
Added reusable_program_contract to reduce file-load growth by requiring reusable runners/program specs before bespoke execution machinery.
Added implementation_behavior_contract for deterministic CLI/code-generation defaults and machine-resolved situational behavior rules.
Added execution identity policy declaring phase-scoped step_id values and globally unique phase_step_key references.
Added template placeholder policy separating reusable template scaffold placeholders from concrete plan-instance validity rules.
Added generated-artifact rebuild rule requiring inventory, indexes, and evidence to be regenerated from source documents rather than manually patched.

template_placeholder_policy.validator_rule: template validators may allow declared scaffold placeholders; plan-instance validators must reject unresolved placeholders.

critical_constraint.name: NO_IMPLIED_BEHAVIOR
rules: Every rule, gate, and contract must be expressible as: config entry, executable command, evidence artifact path, pass/fail condition
Items that cannot be expressed this way MUST be marked NOT_AUTOMATABLE and escalated to manual steps
EVERY STEP must have explicit inputs/outputs/invariants defined
Executable steps must not leave pattern_id blank or null; when no approved pattern exists, create and validate a PATTERN_CREATION path before implementation.
Behavior may only be bound through registered executors plus approved spec/config/schema artifacts; prompt-only or ad hoc runtime behavior is forbidden.
Executable steps may not invoke unregistered executors or bypass GATE-CFG-001 through GATE-CFG-006.
External technical detail required for correct implementation MUST be bound via referenced_context_artifacts.
Every planned file mutation MUST be declared before execution in artifact_intent_manifest.
Planned metadata MUST be marked as intent, never as observed reality.
Only stable, non-derived, pre-execution-known metadata may appear in planned_registry_projection.
Observed runtime facts MUST be written only after execution and reconciliation.
A step MAY NOT rely on unstated context outside plan-declared referenced artifacts.
Contracts are the mandatory abstraction mechanism at governed boundaries; raw tool calls, raw physical paths, informal handoffs, and hidden prompt behavior are not valid boundary abstractions.
Important or shared repo paths MUST be referenced by registered path keys and resolved through the declared path resolver.

New bespoke runners, validators, orchestration scripts, report parsers, or healing scripts are forbidden unless reusable_program_contract proves no existing reusable program type can support the work.
Every governed boundary MUST declare an abstraction_binding that identifies input contracts, output contracts, path keys, resolver usage, and validation evidence.
Every governed path mutation MUST declare whether path abstraction is required, the path_key when required, and the resolver evidence used to prove resolution.
If abstraction, path resolution, or reusable-program applicability cannot be mechanically verified, the step MUST fail closed or be marked NOT_AUTOMATABLE with manual review evidence.
step_id values are phase-scoped; global execution references MUST use phase_step_key formatted as {phase_id}/{step_id}.

Blank/null scaffold placeholders are allowed only in reusable template mode when explicitly covered by template_placeholder_policy; concrete plan instances MUST resolve or mark each such field NOT_AUTOMATABLE with evidence.

Generated artifacts such as inventory.json, semantic indexes, and evidence reports MUST be rebuilt from source documents; manual mutation of generated artifacts is forbidden unless the generator is unavailable and the exception is recorded.

steps: Read the entire template to understand the structure
Fill in all sections with project-specific details
Define step-level contracts for EVERY execution step
Generate complete file manifest with read/write permissions for each phase AND step
Generate executable commands for every gate and validation
Define regex patterns for all expected outputs
Create decision ledger entries for all choices made
Output ONE complete plan document following this structure

step_level_contracts.description: Machine-checkable contracts at individual step granularity
features: inputs: explicit file/data requirements with must_exist checks
outputs: guaranteed artifacts with path policies
invariants: things that must remain true during step execution
failure_modes: enumerated error codes with remediation
allowed_paths: glob patterns for permitted access
forbidden_paths: glob patterns for denied access
read_only_paths: paths that cannot be modified
enforcement via git hooks or pre-execution checks

file_manifest.description: Complete list of all files that will be touched during execution
file_manifest.purpose: Enable change impact analysis, conflict detection, parallel execution safety, and review scoping

checks: All paths are absolute or relative to repo root
No duplicate entries within same phase or step
Write permissions don't overlap across parallel phases/steps
All files marked for write have corresponding read permission
File scopes respect worktree isolation boundaries
Step-level scopes are subsets of phase-level scopes

rules: Each worktree writes ONLY inside its own root
Shared artifacts written only by orchestrator after validation
No direct commits to base_ref; merge via squash-merge
Worktree must pass all validation gates before merge
Step-level file scopes enforced within worktree

merge.command: git checkout {base_ref} && git merge --squash {branch_name} && git commit -m '[{phase}] {summary}'

priority_rules: Phases with more dependents have higher priority
Phases in critical path have higher priority

enforcement.rule: Phase CANNOT transition to next phase unless minimum level passed for ALL steps

PH-00.purpose: Bootstrap project metadata and plan skeleton
invariants: No code files modified outside planning docs
postconditions: PLAN.md created with section headers A-K

PH-02.purpose: Define validation gates and evidence paths
invariants: Every gate has command + regex + evidence
preconditions: PLAN.md includes sections H and I

PH-03A.purpose: Implement core changes in an isolated worktree
invariants: All writes confined to worktree root
postconditions: Patch diff exists and is non-empty

failure_modes.remediation: Check write permissions on repo root

STEP-001.purpose: Parse PLAN.md and extract gate requirements

failure_modes.remediation: Verify PLAN.md has sections H and I

validation_gates.purpose: Repo structure is consistent
Validation gates YAML is valid
Step Contracts Complete
Assumptions Documented
Unit tests pass
Coverage meets minimum threshold
File mutations have deterministic execution methods
Every executable step has pattern_id, executor_id, spec_file, config_schema_file
Behavior supplied by external spec/config, not step prose or custom commands
Reject bespoke commands where a registered pattern exists for the task kind
Reject executor_ids not present in executor_registry
Implementation steps cannot be scheduled before PATTERN_CREATION work when no pattern exists
Executor + spec + config + expected outputs are mechanically linked
Referenced context completeness: all externally required technical artifacts are declared, scoped, and resolvable.
Context authority resolution: conflicting referenced artifacts are resolved by declared authority rules.
Artifact intent completeness: required create, edit, and remove artifacts are declared before execution.
No undeclared mutation intent: step-level mutations are represented in artifact_intent_manifest.
Planned registry projection validity: planned projections contain only pre-execution-known fields.
Planned vs observed artifact reconciliation: actual outputs match artifact intent records or are explained.
Planned projection vs observed registry reconciliation: registry writes match planned projections or are explained.
Abstraction governance contract is present, scoped, and valid.
Every governed step has a valid abstraction_binding.
Governed artifact paths use registered path keys.
All declared path keys resolve through the configured resolver.
No new hard-coded governed paths are introduced.
Reusable program contract blocks new bespoke execution machinery unless an exception is approved.
Every automation step maps to a reusable program type or approved exception.
Implementation behavior defaults and situational rules resolve deterministically.

ci_parity.notes: All commands must run in CI without interactive prompts
requirements: All inputs via config files or env vars

what_was_delivered.title: Phase Plan v3.3 with Context Binding and Reconciliation Features

factors: File scope enforcement with hard boundaries (phase + step level)
Ground truth levels with mechanical pass/fail (phase + step level)
Self-healing with bounded retries and convergence detection (step-aware)
Metrics with quantified targets (phase + step level)
Step-level contracts with cryptographic evidence chain
Per-step file scoping with automatic enforcement

when_receiving_project_request: Read the user's request carefully and paste it verbatim into original_request
Classify the project using the decision tree (count files, LOC, determine complexity)

For every task kind: search the pattern registry for a matching approved pattern; if found, bind the task; if not found, create a PATTERN_CREATION step before any implementation step for that task kind; record pattern_resolution_decision in task_analysis before proceeding
Determine average steps per phase based on complexity classification
Transform vague requirements into quantified targets with validation commands and regex
For every assumption: assign impact score, define validation method, write executable command
Define phases based on complexity, identify parallel execution opportunities
Break down EACH phase into concrete steps with step_id (STEP-001, STEP-002, etc.)
For EACH step: define inputs, outputs, invariants, preconditions, postconditions, file_scope, failure_modes
Generate complete file manifest: list ALL files to be touched with read/write/create permissions per phase AND per step
Validate file manifest for conflicts: ensure no write collisions in parallel phases or steps
For each validation gate: write exact command, define expect/forbid regex, specify evidence path, associate with step_id if applicable
For self-healing: only include relevant failure types, list 2-4 allowed actions, define abort conditions, mark if step_context_required
For metrics: define 8-12 metrics with quantified targets at both phase and step level
Generate decision log entries for all choices made
Output ONE complete document with all sections filled in
do_not: Leave placeholders like TODO or TBD
Write vague success criteria (works well, good enough)
Use subjective pass/fail (looks correct, seems fine)
Forget to mark NOT_AUTOMATABLE when appropriate
Create phases without breaking them into steps
Forget to link gates to steps via step_id field
Do not leave pattern_id blank on executable steps
Do not propose bespoke scripts where a registered pattern exists
Do not treat prose instructions as a substitute for config/schema
Do not start implementation before pattern creation/validation when no pattern exists
Do not bind behavior through runtime prompt overrides
Do not create or invoke unregistered executors
Do not place unique behavior in arbitrary code outside approved extension slots
remember: Every rule = config + command + evidence + pass/fail condition
Every step must have step_id for tracking
File scopes nest: step scopes must be subsets of phase scopes
Evidence paths include phase AND step_id
Metrics track both phase-level and step-level progress
All automatable behavior = executor + spec/config + schema + evidence
Pattern gap => create pattern first, then implement
Executable step without validated pattern = invalid plan
Configuration is the behavior surface; code is the engine surface

execution_identity_policy.global_uniqueness_rule: phase_step_key values must be globally unique across the plan; bare step_id values must be unique only within their parent phase_id.

gate_execution_stage_separation.rule: Do not overload phase_id with execution-stage labels when a gate is not bound to a concrete phase.

artifact_intent_manifest.description: Pre-execution declaration of all intended file mutations and deliverables.

records.purpose: Initial plan document produced by the example plan-document pattern.
Gate requirement draft produced by the example gate-extraction pattern.

reusable_program_ref.required_when: artifact is produced by reusable program

execution_baseline.description: Baseline system state before execution begins

policies: No two concurrent steps may modify the same file path
Steps without explicit independence proof execute serially

rollback_plan.description: Global rollback procedures for phases and steps
rollback_plan.global_procedure: NOT_AUTOMATABLE: define project-specific global rollback procedure before execution

evidence_requirements.description: Required evidence artifacts per phase and step

canonicalization_contract.description: Defines canonical representation of tasks and results

merge_policy.description: Policy for merging worktrees back to the base branch

platform_profile.notes: Template defaults assume a POSIX-compatible shell. Add explicit Windows command variants before claiming Windows support.

abstraction_governance.law_statement: Contracts are the only valid abstraction at governed boundaries. Boundary behavior must be expressed through versioned input/output contracts, path keys, schema-backed envelopes, and registered executors rather than raw calls or prose.

reusable_program_contract.purpose: Prevent file-load growth by requiring reusable deterministic program types and data-only specs before new execution machinery is introduced.
